title: No Project Manager Assigned
insight: Project has no assigned PM. All coordination handled ad-hoc by department heads with competing priorities.
detectionSignal: Project brief lists no PM role; org chart shows no dedicated project function
prevention: IF no PM is assigned before planning begins THEN halt planning and escalate hiring as a prerequisite

title: Decision Debt Accumulation
insight: 4 critical decisions remain unmade after full analysis cycle. Each deferred decision generates 2-3 downstream blockers. Pattern: decisions deferred to avoid conflict.
detectionSignal: Number of pending governance decisions increases across phases; blocker count grows faster than resolution rate
prevention: IF governance decisions are deferred past their deadline THEN escalate to board level with cost-of-delay analysis

title: Unsigned Project Charter
insight: Project charter drafted but unsigned. No formal authority to proceed. Team operating on verbal approvals only.
detectionSignal: Charter document exists but signature field is empty; no board resolution or approval email on file
prevention: IF charter is unsigned after 2 weeks THEN escalate to board level as a go/no-go gate

title: Single Point of Governance Failure
insight: CFO is decision-maker for 3 of 4 governance items. If unavailable or unwilling, entire project blocks. No delegation mechanism exists.
detectionSignal: Escalation matrix shows >50% of decisions route to single individual; no deputy or delegation protocol
prevention: IF single stakeholder owns >2 critical decisions THEN establish delegation protocol and deputy decision-maker

title: Governance Mechanism Absence
insight: No steering committee, no change control board, no formal escalation path. Decisions have no forum for resolution.
detectionSignal: No evidence of governance forum meetings, minutes, or decision logs in project documentation
prevention: IF no governance forum exists by project week 2 THEN establish steering committee with monthly cadence as minimum

title: Analysis Without Authority
insight: Detailed planning proceeds without organizational authority to act on it, creating an illusion of progress while blockers remain.
detectionSignal: Planning outputs grow in detail while zero execution tasks are authorized; increasing gap between plans and approvals
prevention: IF planning phase exceeds 4 weeks without execution authorization THEN trigger mandatory go/no-go gate review

title: Cost of Indecision Unquantified
insight: Estimated cost of each week of continued indecision: $15K direct costs plus $35K opportunity cost. Stakeholders unaware of this burn rate.
detectionSignal: Decision delays exceed 2 weeks with no cost-of-delay calculation presented to stakeholders
prevention: IF decisions are delayed >1 week THEN present cost-of-delay analysis to decision-maker within 48 hours

title: Zero Risks Closed
insight: 22 risks identified but zero closed or mitigated. Risk register growing without active management. Indicates absent risk governance.
detectionSignal: Risk register entry count increases across phases while closure count remains at zero
prevention: IF zero risks are closed after 2 analysis phases THEN escalate risk management gap as a critical finding

title: Budget Gap Pattern
insight: Bottom-up estimate ($1.55M) exceeds client budget ($850K) by 45%. Gap was detectable from the brief but not flagged by the client.
detectionSignal: Bottom-up estimate exceeds top-down budget by >20%; no contingency allocation in client budget
prevention: IF budget gap exceeds 20% THEN require scope-budget reconciliation workshop before detailed planning

title: No Contingency Allocated
insight: Client budget includes zero contingency reserve. Industry standard for migration projects is 15-25%.
detectionSignal: Budget breakdown shows no line item for contingency or management reserve
prevention: IF project budget has zero contingency THEN flag as critical risk and recommend 15% minimum reserve

title: Scope Not Confirmed
insight: Full migration vs phased approach not decided. Two parallel schedules maintained: compressed (25 weeks) vs phased (55 weeks). 2.2x variance.
detectionSignal: Multiple schedule variants maintained in parallel; scope decision listed as 'pending' in project documents
prevention: IF scope is not baselined by end of initiation phase THEN block entry to planning phase

title: Unverified Assumptions
insight: 3 critical assumptions about legacy system compatibility remain unverified. Planning proceeds on unvalidated foundations.
detectionSignal: Assumption log shows critical assumptions with status 'unverified' beyond initiation phase
prevention: IF critical assumptions are unverified after initiation THEN require validation sprint before planning proceeds

title: No Data Quality Assessment
insight: Migration plan assumes data is clean and migration-ready. No data quality audit has been performed. Legacy HR system is 15 years old.
detectionSignal: Migration plan references 'data migration' without a preceding data quality assessment deliverable
prevention: IF data migration is in scope THEN require data quality audit as a prerequisite to migration planning

title: No Rollback Plan
insight: No rollback procedure defined for migration failure. If go-live fails, there is no documented path back to the legacy system.
detectionSignal: Deployment plan has no rollback section; go-live checklist has no failure scenario procedures
prevention: IF migration project has no rollback plan THEN block go-live approval until rollback is documented and tested

title: Change Management Absent
insight: No organizational change management plan exists. Migration assumes users will adopt new system without structured support.
detectionSignal: Project plan has no change management workstream; no training budget allocated; no adoption metrics defined
prevention: IF project impacts >50 users THEN require change management plan as a planning phase deliverable

title: Stakeholder Misalignment
insight: CFO focused on cost containment, CTO on technical completeness, HR Director on minimal disruption. No aligned success criteria.
detectionSignal: Stakeholder interviews reveal conflicting definitions of project success; no shared success criteria document
prevention: IF key stakeholders define success differently THEN facilitate alignment workshop before planning begins

title: Sponsor Disengagement
insight: Key decision-makers not engaging with analysis outputs. Reports produced but not reviewed or acted upon.
detectionSignal: Reports sent but no acknowledgment; decision deadlines pass without response; meeting invitations declined
prevention: IF sponsor does not acknowledge reports within 5 business days THEN escalate engagement gap to next authority level

title: Competing Departmental Priorities
insight: HR team cannot dedicate more than 20% capacity. IT team has 3 concurrent projects. Neither team has project as top priority.
detectionSignal: Resource allocation shows <30% availability for key teams; project not listed as top priority in departmental OKRs
prevention: IF project requires >40% of a team's capacity THEN require department head sign-off on resource commitment before planning

title: No Communication Cadence
insight: No regular stakeholder alignment forum exists. Decisions deferred because there is no formal escalation path or meeting cadence.
detectionSignal: No recurring governance meetings on stakeholder calendars; no communication plan in project documentation
prevention: IF no communication cadence is established by end of initiation THEN create and enforce minimum bi-weekly stakeholder meetings

title: End-User Voice Missing
insight: Requirements gathered from leadership only. No input from end-users (HR staff, managers) who will use the system daily.
detectionSignal: Stakeholder register has no entries below manager level; requirements traceable only to leadership interviews
prevention: IF system will be used by >20 people THEN include end-user representatives in requirements gathering

title: Legacy System Dependencies
insight: HR system tightly coupled with 3 downstream systems. Migration requires coordinated cutover or parallel running period not in current plan.
detectionSignal: System architecture diagram shows undocumented integrations; no integration test plan in project scope
prevention: IF migrating a system with >2 integrations THEN require integration mapping and cutover strategy before planning

title: No Vendor Evaluation
insight: Target cloud platform appears pre-selected without formal evaluation. No vendor comparison or selection criteria documented.
detectionSignal: Single vendor named in project brief without evidence of comparative evaluation or selection process
prevention: IF vendor selection exceeds $100K in licensing THEN require documented vendor evaluation with minimum 2 alternatives

title: Compliance Underestimation
insight: GDPR and local data regulations mentioned in brief but not reflected in effort estimates. Compliance work typically adds 15-20% to migration scope.
detectionSignal: Compliance requirements listed in constraints but no corresponding tasks or budget in WBS
prevention: IF project involves personal data migration THEN add compliance workstream with dedicated budget allocation

title: No Technical Proof of Concept
insight: Migration approach assumes technical feasibility without a proof of concept. No prototype or pilot has been executed.
detectionSignal: Technical approach section references 'standard migration methodology' without project-specific validation
prevention: IF migration involves legacy systems >10 years old THEN require technical POC before committing to schedule

title: Circular Dependencies in Execution
insight: Three circular dependency risks identified where two tasks each wait on the other's output. Requires PM intervention to break cycles.
detectionSignal: Dependency graph analysis reveals cycles; tasks with mutual predecessors in WBS
prevention: IF dependency map contains cycles THEN resolve with PM-led dependency breaking session before sprint planning

title: 40% Resource Capacity Gap
insight: Peak demand 12 FTEs vs available 7 FTEs. 40% gap with no resource acquisition plan because budget is unconfirmed.
detectionSignal: Resource histogram shows demand exceeding supply by >25% during any sprint; no hiring plan in project documents
prevention: IF resource gap exceeds 25% THEN require resource acquisition plan as a condition of planning phase exit

title: Analysis Cost Unbudgeted
insight: Project analysis itself consumes resources without formal budget approval. Team time spent on planning is an untracked cost.
detectionSignal: Team members billing time to project with no approved budget code; EV shows actual cost with no planned value
prevention: IF project analysis exceeds 2 weeks THEN require budget allocation for planning phase before continuing

title: No Training Budget
insight: Budget allocation includes zero for training and change management. System migration success requires user adoption support.
detectionSignal: Budget breakdown has no line items for training, documentation, or change management
prevention: IF project affects >50 end users THEN allocate minimum 5% of budget to training and change management

title: Vendor Cost Uncertainty
insight: Vendor licensing estimated at $210K but based on list pricing. No negotiation or volume discount explored. Implementation services not quoted.
detectionSignal: Budget uses vendor list prices without negotiation documentation; no vendor quote on file
prevention: IF vendor costs exceed $100K THEN require formal vendor quote before including in project budget

title: Optimism Bias in Timeline
insight: Compressed schedule has 3 days total float. Any single task delay cascades to project delay. Team believes they can execute with zero margin.
detectionSignal: Critical path analysis shows total float <5% of project duration; schedule assumes no rework or delays
prevention: IF total float is <10% of project duration THEN flag schedule as fragile and require risk-adjusted timeline

title: Belief Decisions Will Self-Resolve
insight: Team assumes governance decisions will be made 'when needed.' No evidence of active decision-seeking behavior.
detectionSignal: Decisions listed as 'pending' across multiple phases without escalation actions or follow-up records
prevention: IF governance decision misses deadline THEN assign dedicated owner to drive resolution within 5 business days

title: Completeness Bias in Brief
insight: 9-item brief assumed to capture all requirements. Analysis found 30 requirements — 3.3x what was explicitly stated. Brief completeness was overstated.
detectionSignal: Extracted requirements count exceeds brief item count by >2x; requirements emerge from implied rather than explicit statements
prevention: IF project brief contains <15 explicit requirements THEN assume 2-3x expansion during analysis and plan accordingly

title: Go-Live Confidence Overestimated
insight: Stakeholders express verbal confidence in project success despite 8/10 gate criteria failing. Disconnect between evidence and perception.
detectionSignal: Stakeholder confidence survey scores >60% while objective gate criteria show <30% readiness
prevention: IF stakeholder confidence exceeds objective readiness score by >30 points THEN present evidence-based reality check

title: Timeline Pressure from Vendor Contract
insight: Existing vendor contract termination window closes Q3 2026. Missing this window adds $180K in renewal costs and 12-month delay.
detectionSignal: External deadline (contract, regulatory, market) creates hard constraint not reflected in schedule buffer
prevention: IF external deadline exists THEN build schedule backward from deadline with minimum 15% buffer

title: Parallel Workstream Assumption
insight: Compressed schedule assumes 4 parallel workstreams. This requires a PM coordinating full-time — a role that doesn't exist.
detectionSignal: Schedule shows parallel workstreams exceeding available coordination capacity; no PM role in team structure
prevention: IF schedule requires >2 parallel workstreams THEN verify dedicated coordination capacity exists before baselining

title: No Schedule Buffer for Decisions
insight: Neither schedule option includes buffer for governance decision-making time. Both assume instant decisions at gate points.
detectionSignal: Gate review milestones have zero-duration tasks; no decision buffer between phases
prevention: IF schedule has gate reviews THEN add minimum 1-week decision buffer after each gate

title: All Critical Defects Are Governance Gaps
insight: 4 critical defects identified in validation — all 4 map directly to unmade governance decisions. Zero critical technical defects exist. The project's analysis quality is high; it's the organizational wrapper that fails.
detectionSignal: All critical defects trace to governance decisions rather than technical issues
prevention: IF all critical defects are governance-related THEN escalate governance resolution as the sole priority before further analysis

title: Analysis-Without-Authority Anti-Pattern
insight: Detailed planning proceeded without organizational authority to act on it. Six agents analyzed the same root cause with increasing precision — but no analysis can substitute for the 4 decisions leadership must make.
detectionSignal: Multiple analysis phases produce consistent findings but no action is taken; analysis depth increases while decision count stays at zero
prevention: IF analysis has identified the same root cause across 3+ phases THEN halt analysis and escalate to decision-makers with a clear action framework

title: Decision Sprint Recommended as Intervention
insight: The most effective intervention is a single 'Decision Sprint' — a focused 3-hour steering committee session to resolve all 4 governance decisions simultaneously. Cost of continued indecision: ~$50K/week.
detectionSignal: Multiple interdependent decisions remain pending; each decision unblocks downstream decisions
prevention: IF 3+ governance decisions are pending simultaneously THEN schedule a single decision session rather than addressing them sequentially
